close_reason: Fixed FD leaks in segment_recycler.ex and writer.ex by ensuring file descriptors are closed on error paths

comments.text:
## Root Cause Found

Two file descriptor leaks in Shale:

### 1. `segment_recycler.ex:176-184` - `allocate_file/2`
```elixir
def allocate_file(path, size_in_bytes) do
  with {:ok, fd} <- File.open(path, [:write, :binary, :raw, :exclusive]),
       :ok <- :file.allocate(fd, 0, size_in_bytes),  # If this fails, fd leaks!
       :ok <- File.close(fd) do
    {:ok, path}
  else
    {:error, :eisdir} -> raise "not implemented"
    {:error, :enoent} -> {:error, :path_does_not_exist}
  end
end
```

### 2. `writer.ex:27-36` - `open/1`
```elixir
with {:ok, stat} <- File.stat(path_to_file),
     {:ok, fd} <- File.open(path_to_file, [:write, :read, :raw, :binary]),
     :ok <- :file.pwrite(fd, 0, @empty_segment_header) do  # If this fails, fd leaks!
  {:ok, %__MODULE__{...}}
end
```

### Fix
Both need try/after or explicit cleanup in else clauses to ensure fd is closed on any error after File.open succeeds.
comments.author: Jason Allum

title: Investigate file descriptor leak exhausting 1M+ handles
